message:
fix(policy): harden rm -rf regex, fix var-assign boundary, add command tokens

Three targeted improvements to the command classification policy:

1. RM_RF_ROOT regex hardening: Extended pattern to catch 'rm -rf ~/*'
   and 'rm -rf ~/' variants. Previous regex only matched 'rm -rf /'
   and 'rm -rf ~' but missed the trailing slash-star pattern that is
   equally destructive.

2. VAR_ASSIGN regex boundary fix: Changed the character class from
   [^\s] to [^\s;&|<>] so that shell metacharacters (semicolons,
   ampersands, pipes, redirects) correctly terminate variable
   assignments. Previously 'FOO=bar;rm -rf /' would be consumed
   entirely as a variable assignment, hiding the destructive command
   that follows.

3. New COMMAND_TOKENS: Added 12 execution wrapper and system tools
   (time, nohup, nice, timeout, doas, su, stdbuf, taskset, ionice,
   strace, valgrind, watch) that can prefix real commands and should
   be recognized as command candidates.

4. New test assertion verifying the semicolon-delimited case:
   'FOO=bar;rm -rf /tmp' now correctly classified as a command
   candidate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
